name: Steamed White Rice
intro: Never fill your pan more than one-third full or the rice will not cook correctly. If making a larger batch, remember the uncooked rice-to-water volume (not weight) ratio is always 1 measure of rice to 1½ measures of water.
method: Put the uncooked rice in a large bowl and cover with cold water. Swirl the water around with your hands; it will become milky from the rice starch. Pour the water out and add fresh water and repeat until the water is almost clear. About five times should do the job. Leave the rice to soak in the last batch of fresh water for about 30 minutes, then drain.

Place the rice in a saucepan along with 750ml (3¼ cups) of cold water, the salt and ghee or butter in a saucepan. Cover with a tight-fitting lid and bring to a boil over a high heat. As soon as the water boils, remove from the heat and let it sit, lid on, for 40 minutes. Don’t remove the lid. After 40 minutes, your rice will be perfectly done. Using a fork or chopstick, separate the rice grains, stirring very slowly. Basmati rice has a tendency to turn to mush if stirred too vigorously.
tips: Note: SOAKING TIME; SOAKING TIME40 MINS.
